extraction.one_line_summary: HIRATA Pacie won from a 6-rider breakaway at the line, outpositioned WRIGHT Fred who was strongest but led out the sprint from too far
tactical_patterns: WRIGHT Fred strongest but inexperienced, led out sprint from front and got passed in final meters by positioning.
Trek controlled category-1 climb for PEDERSEN to drop BENNETT, prioritizing green jersey over stage hunting.
EVENEPOEL held red jersey through flat stage with minimal team support, Quick-Step saving energy before weekend mountains.

rider_signals.WRIGHT Fred: negative: clearly strongest in break but tactically naïve, slammed handlebars after leading out sprint and finishing second
rider_signals.HIRATA Pacie: positive: 33-year-old with experience beat younger stronger riders purely on positioning confidence, fourth wheel into sprint
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: neutral: Trek working for him on climbs to drop BENNETT for green jersey rather than stage wins despite three second places
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: lost 29 seconds on category-1 climb despite Trek pace-making, green jersey under real threat from better climbers
